nodes.x: ASK ye of the LORD rain in the time of the latter rain; [so] the LORD shall make bright clouds, and give them showers of rain, to every one grass in the field.

CHAPTER X.- God is to be sought unto—He o. saive and restore his SK ye of the Lord "rain in’ the time of the latter rain; ‘so the Lord shall make bright clouds, and give: them showers ‘of rain, to every one grass in the field. 2 For the idols have spoken vanity,
bright clouds—rather, "lightnings," the precursors of rain [MAURER].

For the idols have spoken vanity, and the diviners have seen a lie, and have told false dreams; they comfort in vain: therefore they went their way as a flock, they were troubled, because [there was]…

There are false, as well as true dreams. Jeremiah says on behalf of the Lord, "I have heard what the prophets said, that prophecy lies in my name, saying, I have dreamed, I have dreamed." (Jer. 23:25) And Zechariah (10:2) utters this solemn warning, "For the idols have spoken vanity, and the diviners have seen a lie, and have told false dreams." (George Reynolds and Janne M. Sjodahl, Commentary…
Mine anger was kindled against the shepherds, and I punished the goats: for the LORD of hosts hath visited his flock the house of Judah, and hath made them as his goodly horse in the battle.
Out of him came forth the corner, out of him the nail, out of him the battle bow, out of him every oppressor together.
And they shall be as mighty [men], which tread down [their enemies] in the mire of the streets in the battle: and they shall fight, because the LORD [is] with them, and the riders on horses shall be…
The riders on horses shall be confounded: this is the character of the Jews’ enemies, they came with
And I will strengthen the house of Judah, and I will save the house of Joseph, and I will bring them again to place them; for I have mercy upon them: and they shall be as though I had not cast them…
And [they of] Ephraim shall be like a mighty [man], and their heart shall rejoice as through wine: yea, their children shall see [it], and be glad; their heart shall rejoice in the LORD.
54 They shall rejoice and be glad, And their heart shall exult, And they shall make themselves strong against all the children of the world,
I will hiss for them, and gather them; for I have redeemed them: and they shall increase as they have increased.
And I will sow them among the people: and they shall remember me in far countries; and they shall live with their children, and turn again.
I will bring them again also out of the land of Egypt, and gather them out of Assyria; and I will bring them into the land of Gilead and Lebanon; and [place] shall not be found for them.

I will bring them into the land of Gilead, which was the eastern frontier of the land of Canaan, and Lebanon; this was the north frontier of the land, and both fruitful and pleasant (Poole's Commentary)
And he shall pass through the sea with affliction, and shall smite the waves in the sea, and all the deeps of the river shall dry up: and the pride of Assyria shall be brought down, and the sceptre…
pass...sea with affliction—Personifying the "sea"; He shall afflict the sea (JFB Bible Commentary)
And I will strengthen them in the LORD; and they shall walk up and down in his name, saith the LORD.
